Gene_Accession_Number.reason: The paper explicitly identifies PHT4;4 as AT4G00370 in Results/Fig. 3 and Methods.

Source_Species.reason: The gene was studied in Arabidopsis.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Arabidopsis thaliana is explicitly the study species.

Source_Species_Variety.reason: Experiments use Arabidopsis thaliana ecotype Columbia, Col-0.

Applied_Species.reason: Functional analyses of PHT4;4 were done in Arabidopsis.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: The background line is Col-0, with mutant/complementation analyses in this background.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: The study links PHT4;4 to chloroplastic ascorbic acid transport and to the stay-green/chlorophyll phenotype under Fe/P stress.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: Ascorbic acid is vitamin C; the paper uses the name VITAMIN C and AsA.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Results support that chloroplastic AsA transport modulates ROS homeostasis, PRG expression, and chlorophyll accumulation under Fe/P stress.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: PHT4;4 is described as a chloroplastic AsA transporter; functional implication is specifically transport to chloroplasts.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: PHT4;4 is presented as a member of the PHT4 family/chloroplastic transporter.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: The paper explicitly calls PHT4;4 a chloroplastic transporter.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: The statement that PHT4;4 expression is not affected by Fe deficiency appears in the Discussion with citation to Supplementary Fig. 10A, not clearly shown in main Results text.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: PHT4;4 was tested under iron deficiency, phosphorus deficiency/combined −Fe−P, and exogenous AsA conditions.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Mutant and rescue data support that PHT4;4-dependent chloroplast AsA transport is required to prevent chlorosis and PRG repression under −Fe−P.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: Each listed point is supported by Results: pht4;4 fails to stay green under −Fe−P, complementation restores phenotype, exogenous AsA fails to rescue pht4;4, PRGs are downregulated and ROS elevated in pht4;4 under relevant conditions.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed methods are described and used for PHT4;4: GWAS candidate follow-up, mutant/complementation, chlorophyll, qRT-PCR, AsA, ROS, and AsA supplementation.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: The paper does not explicitly discuss 'no general growth penalty'; it mainly reports the specific chlorosis/stay-green phenotype.

Applied_Promoters.reason: Complementation used genomic DNA containing PHT4;4, implying native promoter context in the complementation line.

Omics_Data.reason: PHT4;4 emerged from GWAS using PRG-expression PC1 and the paper provides RNA-seq context and GEO accession GSE163190.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: This is a forward-looking application inference consistent with Discussion, but not directly tested.

Other_Important_Info.reason: The paper explicitly states PHT4;4 transports Pi and AsA, favors AsA transport as causal, and shows that PHT4;3/5/6, PHR1, and SPX1 do not phenocopy pht4;4.

Gene_Accession_Number.reason: VTC4 is explicitly given as AT3G02870.

Source_Species_Variety.reason: The experiments were conducted in Col-0 background.

Applied_Species.reason: Functional tests of VTC4 were in Arabidopsis.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: Mutant analysis was in Col-0-derived Arabidopsis lines.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: The paper explicitly links VTC4 to ascorbic acid (AsA) biosynthesis.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: Ascorbic acid is vitamin C, explicitly referenced by VITAMIN C genes.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Results support AsA as an antioxidant/ROS-related factor maintaining PRGs and chlorophyll under nutrient stress.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: AsA levels were measured in shoots, and chloroplast function is implicated; however chloroplast localization is indirect, so the wording 'Shoots; functionally implicated in chloroplasts' matches the paper.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: VTC4 is explicitly described as part of the AsA biosynthesis pathway.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: The paper states VTC4 is the final enzyme in the AsA biosynthesis pathway.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: Explicitly stated as the final enzyme/terminal step.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: VTC4 is directly linked to formation of AsA in the pathway.

Enzyme_Name.reason: The paper names the gene/enzyme VITAMIN C4 (VTC4).

Primary_Product.reason: VTC4 is linked to production of ascorbic acid.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: The paper states VTC4 is the final enzyme in AsA biosynthesis, but does not explicitly describe the biochemical reaction in detail.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: It is described as an AsA biosynthesis enzyme.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: Results show VTC4 is induced under −Fe−P and decreased under −Fe+P in shoots.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: The paper gives approximate fold changes: two- to threefold increase under −Fe−P and about twofold decrease under −Fe+P.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: VTC4 was evaluated under Fe deficiency, P-related combined deficiency, and in exogenous AsA rescue context.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: vtc4 mutants remain chlorotic under −Fe−P, supporting a role in promoting AsA-dependent protection against chlorosis.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All three summary points are directly supported by Results and Fig. 3.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: The value explicitly says 'Not established'; the paper does not claim rate-limiting status but does present VTC4 as required final-step enzyme.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: RNA-seq, qRT-PCR, mutant analysis, chlorophyll, AsA quantification, and AsA rescue are all reported.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: The paper states vtc4 accumulates 35% less AsA under +Fe+P and is not chlorotic there; no broader penalty is reported.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: The paper shows genetic/regulatory dependence of VTC4 expression on bZIP58, but does not demonstrate a physical protein-protein interaction.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: This is a plausible application inference, but not directly tested in the paper.

Other_Important_Info.reason: The paper explicitly states that VTC4 induction under −Fe−P is partially dependent on bZIP58 and Fig. 6 presents the effect as possibly direct or indirect.

corrections.reason: The paper shows genetic/regulatory dependence of VTC4 expression on bZIP58, but does not demonstrate a physical protein-protein interaction.

Gene_Accession_Number.reason: bZIP58 is explicitly identified as AT1G13600.

Source_Species_Variety.reason: The study uses Col-0 and corresponding mutant/complemented lines.

Applied_Species.reason: Functional analyses were performed in Arabidopsis.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: Chlorophyll is directly linked to bZIP58 phenotypes, but 'ascorbic acid-associated stay-green phenotype' is indirect and not a terminal metabolite directly controlled by bZIP58 in the same sense.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: Chlorophyll is a tetrapyrrole pigment; this is consistent with the paper's chlorophyll focus.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Results support that bZIP58 maintains PRG expression and chlorophyll accumulation, thereby supporting photosynthesis under nutrient stress.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Chlorophyll/chlorosis phenotypes were assessed in shoots/leaves.

Regulator_Type.reason: bZIP58 is described as a putative/nuclear transcription factor.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: Its name and description identify it as a bZIP transcription factor.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: Nuclear localization is shown in Fig. 4d.

Metabolic_Process_Controlled.reason: The paper supports control of PRG expression/chlorophyll accumulation and partial control of VTC4 expression.

Regulation_Mode.reason: Results support activation of PRGs, partial positive regulation of VTC4, and repression of bZIP58 transcript by ROS/−Fe.

Primary_Regulatory_Targets.reason: The 32 PRGs are the central target set; direct activation was shown for PSBTN, PHOTOSYSTEM II 5 kD, and PTAC16, and VTC4 expression is partially bZIP58-dependent.

Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes.reason: bZIP58 activates PRGs and partially promotes VTC4 expression under −Fe−P.

Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product.reason: Loss-of-function causes constitutive PRG repression/chlorosis and AsA cannot rescue, supporting key downstream regulatory role.

Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs.reason: Fe status, P availability, H2O2/ROS, and AsA treatment all affect bZIP58 expression or placement in the pathway.

Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation.reason: The proposed module is model-based and explicitly not fully established; the paper suggests but does not prove this feedforward logic.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: Results state strong downregulation in shoots by −Fe+P, alleviation under −Fe−P, and weaker downregulation in roots.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: Shoots are the main tissue; AsA prevents repression and H2O2 strongly represses expression.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: All listed treatments were tested for bZIP58.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: bzip58 mutants are constitutively chlorotic with reduced chlorophyll and elevated ROS, and fail to maintain PRG expression.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All four listed findings are directly supported by Results and figures.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed validation approaches are described and used for bZIP58.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: The paper reports constitutive chlorosis/elevated ROS in the mutant and does not report yield data.

Applied_Promoters.reason: The paper reports genomic complementation, p35S::bZIP58::GFP localization, and JUB1 promoter-driven re-expression.

Omics_Data.reason: The study uses RNA-seq (GSE163190) and GWAS on 727 accessions with 1001 Genomes SNP data.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: This is a reasonable implication from the Discussion but not directly tested.

Other_Important_Info.reason: The paper explicitly states that AsA supplementation cannot rescue the bzip58 chlorotic phenotype, placing bZIP58 downstream of AsA.
